copula Monte-Carlo’d over all pairs (βfull-​Σ=0.023\beta_{\textrm{full-}\Sigma}=0.023). The empirical tail exceeds even the nearest-PSD
full-Σ\SigmaGaussian fit by2.25×2.25\times, whose lower tail is asymptotically independent (Props.2,3). (The discredited Pearson-of-indicators calibration would putβsf=0.0016\beta_{\mathrm{sf}}=0.0016, a spurious32×32\times; we exclude it.)Right: resampling poolcomposition(randomkk-model subsets,6060perkk), the tetrachoric underpricing rises monotonically from1.01.0atk=2k{=}2to median2.52.5(55–95%95\%band[2.1,2.7][2.1,2.7]) atk=67k{=}67—size, not which models, drives it. Figure 4:Two regimes across the domains.All-models-wrong rateβ\betaper domain with95%95\%Clopper–Pearson intervals, recomputed from the committed outcome matrices.Ceiling-bounddomains (open-ended math and
code, free-response GPQA) carry a co-failure tailβ>0\beta>0that caps every selection policy at1−β1-\betaand that pairwiseρ\rhounderprices by2.52.5–8.3×8.3\times(tetrachoric);realizability-bounddomains (multiple-choice GPQA, MMLU-Pro) haveβ≈0\beta\approx 0, so the oracle gain is pure resolvable disagreement a router could in principle capture. The same GPQA-Diamond
content sits inbothregimes depending only on answer format (Fig.3).

Figure 5:Pillar A cost–quality frontier (re-graded): the per-query oracle (green) sits just above single-best
(black); cheap models populate the frontier.

Figure 5:Pillar A cost–quality frontier (re-graded): the per-query oracle (green) sits just above single-best
(black); cheap models populate the frontier.

Figure 6:Pillar C: the cascade’s advantage over random-mixing-at-matched-budget collapses to zero as the
verifier’s AUC falls to12\tfrac{1}{2}, confirming Eq. (2).

Figure 7:Pillar B, 455 triplets: unweighted majority-vote gain over the best member vs.ρ\rho; gains are mostly
negative (robust), the positive slope not significant under model-clustering.

negative (robust), the positive slope not significant under model-clustering.

Figure 8:Equal-quality break-even (S=9S{=}9, matched 6-model band): low-ρ\rhoheterogeneous fusion (ρ=0.42\rho{=}0.42)
vs. high-ρ\rhoSelf-MoA (ρ=0.80\rho{=}0.80), both on distinct draws so Self-MoA scales without recycling samples. Under the pre-registered
distinct-draw aggregation the heterogeneous ensemble beats Self-MoA from the information-fairk=3k{=}3onward (∗\ast:
query-bootstrap 95% CI excludes zero), supporting the diversification mechanism at matched quality in this regime.

Figure 9:Pillar A, realizable routing: a held-out learned router (TF-IDF++domain) vs. the cost-aware oracle
(optimal) frontier and single-best, on the multi-domain mix. The learned router barely exceeds single-best (captures∼\sim9% ofGG, CI spans zero) and lies well below the optimal frontier; realizable routing gain is near zero on the 2026 frontier.
